answer:
<think> The Catilinarian conspiracy was a plot to overthrow the Roman Republic in 63 BC. One of the chief figures of this conspiracy was Lucius Sergius Catilina, also known as Catiline. A general who played a critical role in the transformation of the Roman Republic was Gaius Julius Caesar. Caesar was a military leader who rose to power during this time and played a key role in the events leading up to the end of the Roman Republic and the rise of the Roman Empire.

It is known that Caesar and Catiline had a complex relationship. Caesar was initially an ally of Catiline, but they later became political rivals. In 63 BC, Caesar was consul, and he was one of the main accusers of Catiline during the Senate's investigation of the Catilinarian conspiracy. Caesar's actions against Catiline helped to solidify his position as a powerful figure in Roman politics.

Therefore, Lucius Sergius Catilina, the chief figure of the Catilinarian conspiracy, was connected to Gaius Julius Caesar through their political relationship and the events surrounding the Catilinarian conspiracy. </think>

<answer> Gaius Julius Caesar
